answer_text: scalability: take advantage of multi-core, robustness: kernel thread survives in the face of thread failure
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
1. Kernel-level thread could take advantage of multi-processor or multicore processor, while user-level threads are executed by single core.
2. Kernel-level thread could access hardware directly, while user-level can't.
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
more robust:user-level threads would 崩潰 by a failure thread 處理，kernel-level threads run concurrently can avoid.

scalebility:user-level threads are bound to single core; kernel-level threads can run concurrently in multicore, scale up by # of core.
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
robustness: 當kernel thread運行發生錯誤時不會使整個process癱瘓，但user-level thread發生錯誤會讓整個process都無法運行
scalability: kernel thread can take advantage of multi-core processors, more efficiency, but user-level thread can only excutes on single processor
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text: 不會因為有一個thread被block就將整個process block、可以透過multi-thread的方式加速process的執行
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
1. kernel-level threads可以分配在多個processors上面跑，但是user-level threads被限制在單個processor
2. kernel-level threads的scheduling是由os幫忙，所以application不用自己負責scheduling
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
1. 平行化: 不同 kernel-level 的 threads 可以在不同的 cpu core 上面運行達到平行化運算。
2. 便利性: kernel-level threads 直接由 OS 做排程，不需要 user 親自實作。
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
1. Can use the advantage of multicores.
2. Better robustness.
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
1.可以用到CPU多核心的優勢，user-level threads只能在單一CPU上運行
2.user-level threads只要一個thread錯誤可能就會導致整個process壞掉，但kernel-level threds不會，其他threads會繼續運行。
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
1. kernel-level threads can take advanatge of multi-cores, while use user-level threads can use only single core.
2. When one of the thread in a process failed to operate, it won't effect the whole process in kernel-level thread. But in user-level threads, if one of them failed will let the entire process failed.
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
kernel threads can really and correctly make use of multi-core processor, user-level may still bound by single cpu.
kernel threads can survive after ones failure, but user-level may bring the whole process down.
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
1. scability : kernel-level threads can be executed on different CPU on multi-core system, user-level threads are bounded to one process, which limits the scability.
2. about kernel-level threads, when a thread is broken, other threads won't be influenced and keep executing. about user-level threads, when a thread is broken, it will influence or block other threads for execution.
prompt_preview: List two advantages of kernel-level threads over user-level threads.

answer_text:
1. kernel thread能夠在多核cpu上同步進行，而user thread只能綁定在單一個cpu核心

answer_text: Demand paging is a concept when a process try to access to a data. Instead of accessing to the data thanks to the physical address (seen by the memory unit), it uses the logical address to simplify the memory management by the user, to secure the memory between the different users and by abstraction to allow more logical memory for a process than the actual physical memory. Therefore, when a process try to access to a data, it ask the MMU to translate the logical address to the physical address to get from a page, a frame.

answer_text: running state can go to ready state，如果不是preemptive，這樣一來因為需要io而被趕去block的process就會跟被preempt的process搞混在一起，而有preemptive的話，隨時可以由scheduler從ready state 的process中去挑選
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
A preemptive scheduler could stop the running process, move it to ready queue, and select another process to run.
A non-preemptive scheduler can't stop a process. When a process use the CPU, it could always hold it until the process is terminated.
Preemptive scheduling is always useful to prevent a task holding CPU for a long time, block other short and fast process, leading the decreasing of throughput.
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text: non-preeptive必須等待thread執行完畢或主動提出i/o interrupt，preeptive 可以把正在執行的thread 拉下來(run to ready),換上優先度更高的thread，例如最低緩衝時間的scheduling，在確保所有工作都能在時間內完成德情況下,屬於preeptive的最低緩衝時間scheduling能避免因處理單一工作而造成其他工作沒來的及趕上deadline.
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
preemptive根據priority scheduler選擇priority高的process執行，當一個process執行中但有更高priority的process ready，則會換成priority高的process執行，原本的process從running到ready。non-preemptive 會執行當下的process直到結束或需要等待IO，不會強制切換到另一個process。
當有一個需要的cpu time很長，但是deadline很晚的process A，和一個需要的cpu time很短但deadline很早的process B，如process原本在執行A，preemptive 根據priority可中斷A並執行B，使得兩個process都在ddl前完成，non-preemptive因為要先等待A完成，可能錯過B的deadline。
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
preemptive scheduler跟non-preemptive scheduler會的差別在於當現在在執行的process的priority < 現在enter的process，如果是preemptive的話會將目前的process暫停後去做enter的high priority process;；反之non-preemptive會先將目前的prcess執行完成後再去執行high priority process。
example: priority值越小，priority越高
T=0 enter p1 CPU time = 20  priority = 5
T=1 enter p2 CPU time =1 priority = 1
此時如果是preemptive scheduler的話waiting time會比較小
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
preemptive scheduler可以在process跑到一半的時候os主動把他從cpu換下來加入ready queue，狀態會是從running -> ready，而non-preemptive scheduler則不會出現running -> ready的情形，只會有running -> block或是running -> terminated。
在現在的os中，user在邊執行一個自製的program邊移動滑鼠，滑鼠的移動可以即時顯示在螢幕上，program也同時在cpu上面跑，就是因為滑鼠移動打斷了自製program，先讓cpu處理滑鼠移動，再回去處理program，這樣會讓user有更好的os體驗。
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
preemptive: 當 process A 在執行時，有更加符合此排程演算法的 process B 進入 ready 狀態，或是正在執行的 process A 達到特定條件(在RR中執行時間過長等)，此時 process A 會被中斷工作，強制進入 waiting 狀態，並將 CPU 交給其他工作使用。
non-preemptive: process 一旦開始執行，就會執行直到其工作結束或是其自願放棄  CPU。
例子: 若是有一個 process A 為 cpu-bound 執行時間為 500ms，其在剛開始執行工作時，另外有 process B, C, D 為I/O-bound 執行時間為 20ms 進入 ready 狀態，此時 preemptive scheduling (例如最短剩餘時間演算法)會中斷 process A 的工作，將 CPU 交給 process B, C, D 使用。
此例子的優點為: responsiveness提高了，user 比較不會在輸入指令後等待過久，而 CPU-bound 的工作則可以慢慢做。
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
Preemptive: 可以強制把一個正在執行中的 process 的 CPU 資源釋放掉。
Non-preemptive: 只要 process 拿到 CPU 資源，就一定會執行完。

prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
A non-preemptive scheduler uses scheduling algorithms such as First Come First Serve (FCFS), Shortest Job First (SJF), and Longest Job First in which a process cannot get interrupted.

A non-preemptive scheduler uses scheduling algorithms such as Priority Scheduling and Round Robin. Here, a process can get interrupted move from running state back to ready state. This is beneficial when a high-priority task is requested and should be implemented first. Using an algorithm like Round Robin would allow fairness for process execution.

prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
If a process is still running on CPU, preemptive scheduler can stop the running process and change other processes to run, while non-preemptive process let the executing process finish its task.
If a task has run for a long time and probably never stop, it is better to use preemptive scheduler that other process will have a chance to execute and the throughput may get higher.
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
If there is a process that has higher priority than the process is executing, the preemptive scheduler allows the higher priority process take the CPU from the executing process although it's not finish its time. The non-preemptive scheduler will wait for the executing process finish its time, and then decide the next executed process.
Example: process A has lower priority, 40 CPU burst time and it is executing, process B has higher priority, 2 CPU burst time.
If it's the preemptive scheduler, it will let process B execute first, so process B have 0 waiting time.
But if it's the non-preemptive scheduler, process B will wait for process A finished, and have a longer waiting time. So the preemptive scheduling is beneficial.

prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
In preemptive, if there has higher priority job, the job that now is running will stop and run the job that has higher priority. In non-preemptive, it will finish the job that now is running.

for example, there are process A arrive at 0(ms) run 30(ms), B arrive at 3 run 5, C arrive at 10 run 7.
we use shortest job first and shortest release time first.
for non-preemptive, 0-30 run A, 30-35 run B, 35-42 run C. Waiting time = (0-0)+(30-3)+(35-10) = 52
for preemptive, 0-3 run A, 3-8 run B, 8-10 run A, 10-17 run C, 17-42 run A. Waiting time = (8-3)+(17-10)=12
preemptive has shorter waiting time.

prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
A preemptive scheduler could stop the running process, move it to ready queue, and select another process to run.
A non-preemptive scheduler can't stop a process. When a process use the CPU, it could always hold it until the process is terminated.
Preemptive scheduling is always useful to prevent a task holding CPU for a long time, block other short and fast process, leading the decreasing of throughput.

prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
preemptive: 當 process A 在執行時，有更加符合此排程演算法的 process B 進入 ready 狀態，或是正在執行的 process A 達到特定條件(在RR中執行時間過長等)，此時 process A 會被中斷工作，強制進入 waiting 狀態，並將 CPU 交給其他工作使用。
non-preemptive: process 一旦開始執行，就會執行直到其工作結束或是其自願放棄  CPU。
例子: 若是有一個 process A 為 cpu-bound 執行時間為 500ms，其在剛開始執行工作時，另外有 process B, C, D 為I/O-bound 執行時間為 20ms 進入 ready 狀態，此時 preemptive scheduling (例如最短剩餘時間演算法)會中斷 process A 的工作，將 CPU 交給 process B, C, D 使用。
此例子的優點為: responsiveness提高了，user 比較不會在輸入指令後等待過久，而 CPU-bound 的工作則可以慢慢做。

prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
A non-preemptive scheduler uses scheduling algorithms such as First Come First Serve (FCFS), Shortest Job First (SJF), and Longest Job First in which a process cannot get interrupted.

A non-preemptive scheduler uses scheduling algorithms such as Priority Scheduling and Round Robin. Here, a process can get interrupted move from running state back to ready state. This is beneficial when a high-priority task is requested and should be implemented first. Using an algorithm like Round Robin would allow fairness for process execution.

prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
If a process is still running on CPU, preemptive scheduler can stop the running process and change other processes to run, while non-preemptive process let the executing process finish its task.
If a task has run for a long time and probably never stop, it is better to use preemptive scheduler that other process will have a chance to execute and the throughput may get higher.
prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
If there is a process that has higher priority than the process is executing, the preemptive scheduler allows the higher priority process take the CPU from the executing process although it's not finish its time. The non-preemptive scheduler will wait for the executing process finish its time, and then decide the next executed process.
Example: process A has lower priority, 40 CPU burst time and it is executing, process B has higher priority, 2 CPU burst time.
If it's the preemptive scheduler, it will let process B execute first, so process B have 0 waiting time.
But if it's the non-preemptive scheduler, process B will wait for process A finished, and have a longer waiting time. So the preemptive scheduling is beneficial.

prompt_preview: What is the difference between a preemptive scheduler and a non-preemptive scheduler? Provide an example scenario where

answer_text:
In preemptive, if there has higher priority job, the job that now is running will stop and run the job that has higher priority. In non-preemptive, it will finish the job that now is running.

for example, there are process A arrive at 0(ms) run 30(ms), B arrive at 3 run 5, C arrive at 10 run 7.
we use shortest job first and shortest release time first.
for non-preemptive, 0-30 run A, 30-35 run B, 35-42 run C. Waiting time = (0-0)+(30-3)+(35-10) = 52
for preemptive, 0-3 run A, 3-8 run B, 8-10 run A, 10-17 run C, 17-42 run A. Waiting time = (8-3)+(17-10)=12
preemptive has shorter waiting time.

當process 1需要運行10ns 而process 2運行只要1ns, 在preemptive schduling下可以先進行process 2再進行process 1, mean delay time只有0.5ns, 但在non-preemptive schduling下只能先進行process 1再進行process 2, mean delay time則有5ns, preemtive schduling效率會比較好

prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
Priority inversion is the problem that the low priority task hold a resource which is needed by a high priority task. Causing the high priority task can't be executed.
The scenario where busy waiting lead to it is that. Since the high priority task uses CPU more frequently, the low priority task may not release the resource. When the high priority task uses CPU, it always stays in the busy loop and not really doing anything.
Priority inheritance solve this issue by temporary increase the priority of the low priority task to max priority. It let the task could be finished first. Once the task release the resource, the priority of the task is back to the original value.

prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
Priority inversion happens when a processus of lower priority is preventing a processus of higher priority from executing. 
Process A is executing and enters a critical section, therefore "locking" it. Before A has time to release it, process B, with higher priority than A, starts executing. Since B has a higher priority than A, the processor executes B in priority, but B needs to enter the CS that A is currently occupying. B is therefore going to enter spinlock, waiting to be able to enter the CS, while A cannot execute and release it since B is occupying the processor. 
Priority inheritance is when a process that is currently in a CS becomes of the highest priority between all fo the processes waiting for that CS. The execution and the release of the CS therefore becomes the priorised execution.

Priority inheritance lead to solve this issue because, when a process enters in a Critical section then it obtains the maximum priority of all the process which want to enter in the critical section. When the process go out from the critical section, it gets back is original priority.

answer_text:
Priority inversion is the situation where a low-priority process or thread holds a lock to a critical section, and a high-priority task is unable to complete execution as a result. The high-priority task busy waits for the lock to be released, but instead of sleeping, it continuously runs. Since scheduler keeps choosing the high-priority task, the lock is not released. 
Priority inheritance involves increasing the priority of processes that hold a lock in critical section to maximum priority (aging), and only once the lock is released, do the priority levels return to normal. This helps solve priority inversion.

Priority inheritance defines the lower and upper bound time for a process so that the process in the critical section can complete its execution within a bound time, otherwise preempted for other processes to execute.

answer_text:
Priority inversion happens when a processus of lower priority is preventing a processus of higher priority from executing. 
Process A is executing and enters a critical section, therefore "locking" it. Before A has time to release it, process B, with higher priority than A, starts executing. Since B has a higher priority than A, the processor executes B in priority, but B needs to enter the CS that A is currently occupying. B is therefore going to enter spinlock, waiting to be able to enter the CS, while A cannot execute and release it since B is occupying the processor. 
Priority inheritance is when a process that is currently in a CS becomes of the highest priority between all fo the processes waiting for that CS. The execution and the release of the CS therefore becomes the priorised execution.

answer_text:
Priority inversion is the situation where a low-priority process or thread holds a lock to a critical section, and a high-priority task is unable to complete execution as a result. The high-priority task busy waits for the lock to be released, but instead of sleeping, it continuously runs. Since scheduler keeps choosing the high-priority task, the lock is not released. 
Priority inheritance involves increasing the priority of processes that hold a lock in critical section to maximum priority (aging), and only once the lock is released, do the priority levels return to normal. This helps solve priority inversion.

Priority inheritance defines the lower and upper bound time for a process so that the process in the critical section can complete its execution within a bound time, otherwise preempted for other processes to execute.

Priority Inversion:
Assume there are two progress, A and B, where A has a lower priority than that of B.
A is in the Critical Section but B occupy resources of CPU but also wait for the permission.
Unfortunately, A can't escape from Critical Section because of the priority of using CPU resources, and B also need to wait for the permission and release the space for A.
Priority Inheritance: 
To solve this dilemma, we can temporary promote the priority of A to the same as B, which helps A to escape. After escaping, restore the priority of A.
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
當有較低優先權的thread拿到lock時，又有一個更高優先權的thread進來，此使高優先權的會搶佔cpu，但高優先權的沒有拿到lock因此他也沒辦法執行crital section，而一開始拿到lock的也沒有cpu能夠執行，這種現象稱為priority inversion
解決辦法為暫時提高拿到lock卻優先權較低的thread提高為最高優先權，等他執行完還回lock後再降回原本的
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
就是low priority 的擁有lock，但high priority 在一直等那個lock ，因為high priority 的會優先執行因為一直無法拿到lock所以會busy waiting ,然後連帶著low priority 的也無法執行因為在busy waiting的情況下，讓low priority 的一直拿著lock就會導致priority inversion
用priority inheritance 是讓擁有lock的人暫時將他的priority 提到最高，讓他執行完釋放lock的時候就把他的priority 降回原本的priority
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
Process A is a lower-priority process, and Process B is a higher-priority process. When Process A into CS first, and Process B want to go into CS, but because Process B has higher-priority, CPU run the Process B, Process a cannot get resource to unlock, so Process A cannot leave out CS, Process B cannot go into CS.

Priority inheritance can that which one is CS can have highest priority, so CPU will allocate resources to it first, so priority inversion is solved. When unlock, the priority will change to initial priority.
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
priority inversion: higher-priority task is waiting for the resource (ex. lock) , which is currently held by the lower-priority task.
A task 為 high-priority , B task 為 low-priority. 
A task 一直 loop 等待 B task 釋出 lock,  而 B task 則因 low-priority 所以一直沒有執行, 故無法釋出 lock, 產生spinlock, 導致priority inversion 產生.
priority inheritance can let the priority of the task that is holding the lock become maximum priority.
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text: priority inversion是指當low priority process手上持有high priority process需要的lock，但CPU scheduler永遠會讓high priority process先執行，導致high priority process一直在空轉(spinlock)，要解決此問題，要使用priority inheritance，讓low priority process的優先度暫時提高到最優先，讓他趕快執行完並把lock release出來
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text: Lower priority process擁有lock，並在critical section中執行，導致Higher priority process一直spinlock，無法取得lock進入critical section。可以透過priority inheritance解決，增加lower priority process的priority，使得兩者的priority暫時一致，直到Higher priority process取得lock後就恢復原本的priority。
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text: 當low priority在critical section 裡時，hight priority進來了搶佔了CPU導致hight priority 佔著CPU但沒法進去critical section 而low priority也卡在裡面不能釋放鎖。為了解決這個問題就可以暫時把在critical section 裡的priority拉高能夠優先run 等結束出了critical section再降回原來的priority
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
Priority inversion is a demonstration that high-priority process is waiting for a resources held by low-priority process.

In order to solve priory inversion, priority inheritance will be applied.
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text: 優先權反轉是低優先權進程一直在cpu運算，無法釋放鎖，而高優先進程一直spinlock。這時候priority inheritance 會暫時把低優先權進程提高其優先權，以便讓其趕緊做完釋放鎖，之後回到正常的優先權排程
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
1.當一個low priority process拿到lock時，high priority process開始busy waiting，此時OS會讓high priority process佔有CPU，導致low priority process無法執行無法釋出lock，造成high priority process被low priority process卡住的情況。
2.暫時增加low priority process的priority到最高，執行完釋出lock後再降回原本的priority。
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
When a low-priority process is holding a lock, it may stop a process with high-priority from executing. A low-priority process A is holding a lock, and a high-priority B is busy waiting. When process A is looping, process B might never execute. 
Priority inheritance increase the priority of the process holding a lock and decrease priority back to original priority. Thus, the processes will keep processing in priority order.
prompt_preview: What is priority inversion? Describe a scenario where busy waiting (spinlock) can lead to it. How can priority inheritan

answer_text:
priority inversion:當某優先序高的process要使用某個resource時，該resource被低優先序的process所持有
priority inheritance:讓低優先權的process先暫時繼承高優先權process之priorty，直到該process釋放出reesource才將priority變回原樣

answer_text:
Internal fragmentation and external fragmentation happen when partitioning memory. 
External fragmentation happens when, between two partitions, a hole of a size so small that no process can use it is left. Therefore, a portion of memory becomes unusable and is wasted.
Internal fragmentation happens when a partition is allocated to a process, but is too big for it to use it completely. Since this portion is allocated, another process cannot use it, and it becomes unusable and is wasted.
As pages are fixed-sized, most of the processes simply won't use all of it, which leads to internal fragmentation.

answer_text:
Internal fragmentation and external fragmentation happen when partitioning memory. 
External fragmentation happens when, between two partitions, a hole of a size so small that no process can use it is left. Therefore, a portion of memory becomes unusable and is wasted.
Internal fragmentation happens when a partition is allocated to a process, but is too big for it to use it completely. Since this portion is allocated, another process cannot use it, and it becomes unusable and is wasted.
As pages are fixed-sized, most of the processes simply won't use all of it, which leads to internal fragmentation.

answer_text:
Many-to-One - many user threads can use the same kernel thread to be executed.
One-to-One - one kernel thread is created for each user thread, so only one user thread can use the same kernel thread to be executed. This model allows better utilization of multi-core processors, because kernel manages kernel threads and it can execute them on different cpu cores. However the kernel is not aware of user-level threads, so it can not execute them on different cores, thus many-to-one model can not run truly parallel on a multi-core processors.

prompt_preview: Compare the 'Many-to-One' and 'One-to-One' multithreading models. Which one allows better utilization of multi-core proc

answer_text:
Threads are an execution flow. An user thread cannot use multi-core processors, but entrusting it to a kernel thread allows the kernel thread to use multi-core processors to execute it. 
If we attribute many user threads to a kernel one, he will be able to use the multiple cores to execute them all. If we attribute one thread to a kernel one, he will also be able to use the multiple cores to execute it, but that makes the throughput much more efficient (if we have N user threads, it would be more efficient to use 1 kernel to execute them rather than N ones)

prompt_preview: Compare the 'Many-to-One' and 'One-to-One' multithreading models. Which one allows better utilization of multi-core proc

answer_text:
Threads are an execution flow. An user thread cannot use multi-core processors, but entrusting it to a kernel thread allows the kernel thread to use multi-core processors to execute it. 
If we attribute many user threads to a kernel one, he will be able to use the multiple cores to execute them all. If we attribute one thread to a kernel one, he will also be able to use the multiple cores to execute it, but that makes the throughput much more efficient (if we have N user threads, it would be more efficient to use 1 kernel to execute them rather than N ones)
